purpose: The canonical schema reference for every Synapse data file at {tracker_root}: initialization.json (write-once static plan store with task/agents/waves/chains/history objects), logs.json (timestamped event log with info/warn/error/debug/permission/deviation levels), the master task file at {tracker_root}/tasks/{date}/parallel_{name}.json (authoritative task record), the progress/ directory (per-worker progress files — the 17-field lifecycle schema), master_state.json (master's checkpoint for context-compaction recovery), and metrics.json (post-swarm performance metrics). Specifies write rules for each file: who writes it, when, atomic write semantics, ephemerality, and which files the server watches. The 'Context Savings' table contrasts the new per-dashboard model with the legacy single-status.json model.
gotchas: initialization.json is WRITE-ONCE except for circuit-breaker auto-replanning — the orchestrator may modify it when adding repair tasks (r-suffixed IDs), removing tasks, or updating pending tasks. Never update it during normal execution.

Lifecycle fields (status, started_at, completed_at, summary, assigned_agent, completed_tasks, failed_tasks, overall_status) are DELIBERATELY ABSENT from initialization.json — they live exclusively in progress files. Adding them is an architectural violation.
dashboard_id is required in every progress file write — the server REJECTS files where this field doesn't match the directory name. This prevents dashboard cross-contamination.
Progress files are EPHEMERAL — they exist only during the active swarm and are cleared by the master when initializing a new swarm. Don't rely on them post-completion (use Archive/ snapshots).
master_state.json is NOT watched by the server — purely for master self-recovery after context compaction. It's invisible to the dashboard.
metrics.json is written ONCE at end of swarm and never updated during execution — also not watched by the server.
Workers write the FULL progress file every time (sole-writer model) — no read-modify-write needed. The server uses fs.watch to broadcast SSE 'agent_progress' events.
Severity in deviations[] is exactly one of CRITICAL/MODERATE/MINOR — the deviations.md classification guide is the source of truth.
patterns: Per-dashboard file layout: dashboards/{id}/{initialization,logs,master_state,metrics}.json + progress/{task_id}.json — replaces legacy single-root status.json.
Sole-writer atomic-overwrite for progress files: each worker owns exactly one file, writes the full JSON every update.
fs.watch on progress/ + fs.watchFile on init/logs — different mechanisms for directory vs file watching.
Stat derivation from progress files: dashboard counts files by status field rather than maintaining counters.
Sub-schemas: deviations[] has at/severity/description; logs[] has at/level/msg; milestones[] has at/msg; files_changed[] has path/action; annotations is keyed by relative file path.
conventions: Dashboard IDs: 'ide' (reserved), 6-char hex (default, crypto.randomBytes(3).toString('hex')), or legacy 'dashboardN'.
All timestamps ISO 8601 UTC, captured via `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"`.
task.project_root in initialization.json identifies the target project for multi-project support.
Log levels: info, warn, error, debug, permission, deviation — with 'permission' triggering amber popup and 'deviation' rendered with yellow badge.
